opportunities.rationale: BTC closed $76,557 May 19 (investing.com/Bitfinex historical). Down from $81,052 high May 14 — a 5.7% correction. Continuing to drift lower from prior session $76,990. Crypto F&G at 28 (Fear) vs equity F&G 62.66 (Greed) — divergence historically resolved in BTC's favour over 10-30d horizon. 30-day ETF net inflows +$1.85B. Entry zone tightened to $74k-$78k reflecting current level. Stop $70k (structural). Target $90k (prior support level). Invalidated below $70k if macro deteriorates post-FOMC minutes.
opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter on confirmed hold above $74k after FOMC minutes May 20; avoid entry before macro print.

opportunities.rationale: ETH closed $2,133 May 19 (investing.com historical). Relatively stable — consolidating in $2,100-$2,150 band. Yahoo Finance history shows $2,128.55, investing.com shows $2,133.20 close. Key support $2,100-$2,130. Resistance $2,350 then $2,586. ETH underperforming BTC year-to-date. If BTC stabilizes above $74k post-FOMC, ETH historically follows with amplified move. Invalidated below $1,950 if BTC breaches $70k.
opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter only if BTC confirms hold above $74k; enter ETH in $2,050-$2,150 zone.

opportunities.rationale: SHORT-TERM: FOMC Minutes May 20 (8pm CET). Dovish minutes = risk assets including BTC rally. BTC at $76,557 with Crypto F&G at 28 — depressed sentiment, dovish catalyst could close gap with equity Greed. Entry $75k-$76.5k gives R:R of ~1:2 to $85k target. Stop $72k. LOW conviction: binary event risk. Invalidated immediately if minutes signal hawkish tilt or VIX spikes above 21.
